evals.prompt: My SaaS landing page converts at 1.8% and industry average for my niche (HR software) is around 3-4%. The page has: a headline, 3 feature blocks, customer logos, a pricing section, and a 'Get Started' CTA. What should I look at first?
evals.expected_output: Prioritized CRO analysis covering: above-the-fold value proposition, CTA clarity and placement, trust signal effectiveness, friction reduction, and a ranked list of what to fix first.
assertions: Identifies the above-the-fold section as first priority
Questions the headline's clarity and value proposition specificity
Addresses CTA copy ('Get Started' is weak) and placement
Covers trust signal optimization (logos are good, reviews/numbers even better)
Provides a prioritized list of what to test first

evals.prompt: Should I add a video to my homepage hero section?
evals.expected_output: Nuanced CRO analysis of hero video impact with conditions under which it helps vs. hurts (page speed, autoplay, value clarity), and a recommendation framework.
assertions: Addresses page speed impact of hero videos
Distinguishes between product demo video vs. brand video
Gives a conditional recommendation based on the product type
Mentions A/B testing as the definitive answer

evals.prompt: Write the copy for my pricing page
evals.expected_output: Offers CRO guidance on pricing page structure and conversion optimization, redirects to copywriting skill for the actual copy writing.
assertions: Distinguishes between page optimization (CRO) and copy writing
Offers pricing page CRO principles (decoy, anchoring, recommended plan)
Redirects to copywriting skill for the actual writing
